figures.caption: FIG. 7. Effective energy barrier ∆E in the units of kBTRT as a function of d obtained from Fig. 6(e) for the antisymmetric ferromagnetic bilayer with fixed Ds = 1.7 pJ/m.
FIG. 4. Comparison of the skyrmion energy obtained numerically (open circles) with the prediction of (37) (solid lines): (a) the graph of Ē±,0(msky) vs. κ̄; (b) the graph of 8π − Ē±,0(msky) vs. κ̄.

FIG. 6. Skyrmion radius R (a)-(c) and effective energy barrier ∆E in the units of kBTRT (d)-(f), where we excluded skyrmion solutions with ∆E < 20kBTRT , as functions of the interfacial DMI strength Ds and the single ferromagnetic layer thickness d for: (a,d) antisymmetric ferromagnetic bilayer; (b,e) SAF ; (c,f) ferromagnetic monolayer. In (a) and (d): dimensional versions of the skyrmion radius R = ρ̄sky2 LB and the effective energy barrier ∆E = Ad∆Ē2 are dimensional replots of the data from the skyrmion solutions presented in Figs. 3(a) and 3(e) from Sec. IIIG. In (b) and (e): dimensional versions of the skyrmion radius R = ρ̄sky2,0LB , where ρ̄sky2,0 is given by (38), and effective energy barrier ∆E = Ad∆Ēc,0 2 where ∆Ēc,0 2 is given by (37). In (c) and (f): the dimensional version of the skyrmion radius R = ρ̄sky1 LB and the barrier ∆E = Ad∆Ē1 are dimensional replots of the data from the skyrmion solutions presented in Figs. 5(a) and 5(e) from Sec. IVB. Other parameters in this figure are: A = 20 pJ/m, Ms = 1 MA/m, Ku = 0.9 MJ/m3 and Ks = 0 J/m2. The dashed line in (a) and (d) shows Dbi s,opt vs. d from (44).
FIG. 8. Comparison of the quantity in the left-hand side of (A30) (open circles) with the prediction in the right-hand side (solid line).

FIG. 5. Summary of the numerical results obtained from Mumax3 simulations (see Sec. IVB for details) of a ferromagnetic monolayer with the same parameters as in Fig. 3: (a) the dimensionless radius ρ̄sky1 ; (b) the dimensionless skyrmion energy Ēsky 1 ; (c) the dimensionless collapse energy barrier ∆Ēc 1; (d) the dimensionless bursting energy barrier ∆Ēb 1; (e) the dimensionless effective energy barrier ∆Ē1. The solid line shows κ̄opt 1 (δ̄), while the dashed line shows κ̄b 1(δ̄), governed, respectively, by (43) and (40).

FIG. 3. Summary of the numerical results obtained from Mumax3 simulations (see Sec.IIIG for details) of an antisymmetric ferromagnetic bilayer with A = 20 J/m, Ms = 106 A/m, d = 1 nm, and Ku and D varied in accordance with (34) on a 614.4 × 614.4 nm computational domain with periodic boundary conditions in the plane: (a) the dimensionless radius ρ̄sky2 ; (b) the dimensionless skyrmion energy Ēsky 2 ; (c) the dimensionless collapse energy barrier ∆Ēc 2; (d) the dimensionless bursting energy barrier ∆Ēb 2; (e) the dimensionless effective energy barrier ∆Ē2. The solid line shows κ̄opt 2 (δ̄) and the dashed line shows κ̄b 2(δ̄) governed, respectively, by (28) and (21).
